description: Manifest of all repositories in the Groupon cloud infrastructure workspace

repositories.name: gcp-landingzone
repositories.description: GCP organization baseline — folder hierarchy, project provisioning (~271 projects), IAM, shared VPC, firewall rules, central service accounts, and org policies for the Engineering org
typical_tasks: Provision a new GCP project using the project factory
Add or modify IAM bindings for a service
Create a new folder in the GCP org hierarchy

repositories.name: terraform-gcp-core
repositories.description: GCP networking for Conveyor and Hybrid Boundary — dedicated VPCs, subnets, VPC peering with shared VPCs, Cloud Router, NAT, firewall rules, private DNS zones, and billing budgets
typical_tasks: Add or modify Conveyor VPC subnets
Update firewall rules for edge-proxy or health checks

repositories.name: conveyor_k8s
repositories.description: Conveyor Kubernetes Service — full lifecycle management of EKS (AWS) and GKE (GCP) clusters, 50+ Ansible roles for platform service provisioning, cluster promotion, and AMI baking
typical_tasks: Add or modify an Ansible role for platform services
Change EKS or GKE Terraform configuration
Update cluster manifests for a specific environment or region
Bake a new AMI for EKS nodes

repositories.name: hybrid-boundary
repositories.description: AWS edge proxy infrastructure — Envoy-based Go agent on EC2 generating config via xDS (go-control-plane), Lambda functions (API, DNS, service discovery, traffic shifting), DynamoDB service registry, Step Functions for orchestrated migration. HCM CodecType: AUTO (HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 via ALPN downstream); upstream clusters default to HTTP/1.1.
typical_tasks: Update Terraform for edge proxies, ELB, or API Gateway
Modify Envoy configuration or agent behavior
Deploy to sandbox, staging, or production

repositories.name: hybrid-boundary-gcp
repositories.description: GCP edge proxy infrastructure — Envoy-based Go agent on GCE generating config via xDS (go-control-plane), Cloud Functions (API, DNS handler, traffic shifting), Firestore service registry, Cloud Workflows for orchestrated migration. Same xDS architecture as hybrid-boundary.
typical_tasks: Update Terraform for edge proxies, Firestore, or Cloud DNS
Modify Envoy configuration or agent behavior
Deploy to dev, stable, or production

repositories.name: hybrid-boundary-controller
repositories.description: Kubernetes operator that reconciles HybridBoundaryUpstream CRDs — creates Internal LoadBalancer services (ILB IPs on HB subnet) on both AWS and GCP, optionally creates Istio VirtualServices in gateway mode, and registers endpoints with the Hybrid Boundary API. Manages ~422 HBU objects in production GKE (306 HTTP, 116 HTTPS, ~16 in public namespace).
typical_tasks: Change Hybrid Boundary API client behavior
Generate CRD manifests with make manifests

repositories.name: service-fetcher
repositories.description: Lightweight Go service for Kubernetes service discovery — returns ClusterIP for a given service name, called by Hybrid Boundary Lambdas and Cloud Functions

repositories.name: par-automation
repositories.description: Policy Access Request automation — evaluates PAR requests against data classification rules (C1-C3, SOX, PCI), auto-approves or creates Jira tickets, and applies approved policies to Hybrid Boundary
typical_tasks: Update Jira or Service Portal integration

repositories.name: mtls-sidecar
repositories.description: Envoy-based mTLS sidecar for Hybrid Boundary — init container configures iptables traffic interception, Envoy proxy terminates mutual TLS with HB edge proxies using cert-manager certificates. HTTP/2 opt-in via SIDECAR_ENABLE_HTTP2 (outbound to HB) and SIDECAR_ENABLE_INTERNAL_HTTP2 (inbound to app), both default false. Downstream TLS listeners advertise h2,http/1.1 via ALPN.
typical_tasks: Modify Envoy proxy configuration (TLS, circuit breaking, timeouts)
Add new environment variable support for sidecar behavior
Change TLS cipher suites or protocol versions
Debug traffic interception or certificate issues

repositories.name: api-proxy
repositories.description: Central Java Vert.x proxy for API and web traffic — routes requests by realm and path, handles client identification and rate limiting via Redis. Deployed in two contexts: (1) within the routing-service pod alongside nginx (managed by routing-deployment, no mTLS sidecar), and (2) as a standalone deployment with HBU and mTLS sidecar (managed by cmf-helm-charts). In the routing-service pod, connects to backends via HBU ILB IPs over HTTP/1.1.

repositories.name: proxy-config
repositories.description: JSON configuration for api-proxy — verticle deployment, proxy settings, realms, destinations, and routing config paths for both on-prem and K8s environments

repositories.name: routing-config-production
repositories.description: Production routing rules in Grout .flexi DSL — per-application route definitions, per-country host configs, and layer includes for all production traffic

repositories.name: routing-config-staging
repositories.description: Staging routing rules in Grout .flexi DSL — mirrors production structure with additional experimental routes, used for validating changes before production deployment
typical_tasks: Test new routing rules before production

repositories.name: web-config
repositories.description: Nginx configuration generator (also called Grout in routing context) — Mustache templates rendered from YAML data for virtual hosts, rewrites, access control, and error pages. nginx listens on 443 with TLS and proxies to api-proxy on localhost:9000 using proxy_http_version 1.1. Includes Go tool for Jira redirect automation.
typical_tasks: Add or modify virtual host definitions
Deploy config to on-prem or cloud

repositories.name: routing-deployment
repositories.description: Kustomize-based Kubernetes deployment for the routing service stack — combines api-proxy, proxy-config, routing-config, and web-config into a single pod (4 init containers + 6 runtime containers: nginx, api-proxy, telegraf, logstash-kafka-forwarder, logstash-canary, filebeat). Exposed via Internal LoadBalancer restricted to HB subnet. Receives public traffic from CDN via HB edge proxy.
typical_tasks: Update image tags for routing components
Modify deployment spec or resource limits

repositories.name: cmf-helm-charts
repositories.description: Cloud Migration Factory Helm charts — standard packaging for Groupon services on Kubernetes with charts for generic, Java, JTier, Rails, and I-Tier workloads. Emits HybridBoundaryUpstream CRDs
typical_tasks: Add or modify shared templates in common library

repositories.name: hybrid-boundary-ui
repositories.description: Angular web application providing self-service management of Hybrid Boundary — traffic management, endpoint configuration, and policy access requests
typical_tasks: Add or modify UI components for HB management

repositories.name: cloud-ai-root
repositories.description: AI coordination layer — architecture documentation, repository manifest, dependency graph, routing rules, runbooks, and Cursor workspace configuration for multi-repository AI agent navigation
typical_tasks: Update architecture documentation after infrastructure changes
Add new repositories to the manifest
Add runbooks for new incident types
